title: ranunculus aquatilis subsp. diversifolius
intro: Ranunculus aquatilis subsp. diversifolius, also known as the aquatic buttercup, is a perennial aquatic plant that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and diuretic effects. The aquatic buttercup has been used to treat various health issues, such as kidney stones, urinary tract infections, and skin conditions, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote urine production. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and creams for topical application.
